subject: Project Management
title: The Apollo Program
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Apollo Program.

hook.text: In 1961, JFK promised to land on the moon by decade's end. NASA had no idea how.

buildup.text: At peak, 400,000 people worked on Apollo across 20,000 companies and universities.

discovery.text: NASA invented systems engineering — breaking impossible goals into thousands of subproblems.

twist.text: After the Apollo 1 fire killed three astronauts, the program was completely redesigned.

climax.text: On July 20, 1969, Armstrong stepped onto the moon. The deadline was met with months to spare.

punchline.text: The moonshot was a project management triumph.

quiz.question: What management innovation did NASA create for the Apollo program?
options: Systems engineering — breaking goals into thousands of subproblems
